topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a jarring intrusion into the otherwise quiet afternoon. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. I stared at the profile picture—a blurry, overly confident grin from a decade ago. A slow, heavy sigh escaped my lips, the sound grating in the silence of my room. Who on earth was this? I didn't even bother clicking to view the details. Just a faint, unpleasant tightening settled in my chest, the kind that precedes a headache. I swiped the notification away, hoping it would vanish. Another unnecessary thing demanding my attention.

[story 2]

I was wrestling with a stubborn spreadsheet, the numbers refusing to align, when the alert chimed. My shoulders slumped further, a physical manifestation of deep irritation. I glanced at the screen, and the familiar, unwelcome face materialized. A muscle twitched in my jaw. *Seriously?* I thought, the word tasting like ash. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, creating a barrier between myself and whatever digital nuisance this was. I didn't want to engage. I definitely didn't.

[story 3]

The phone buzzed, vibrating insistently on the desk. I didn't look immediately; I just let it sit there, a small, irritating pebble on the wood. When I finally did glance down, the familiar name flashed across the display. A low growl started in my throat, barely audible. I picked up the device with deliberate slowness, my fingers moving with reluctance. A wave of profound annoyance washed over me, making my skin feel tight. I almost tossed it onto the pile of books, but some internal resistance held me in place.
